# Transition-Candidate Audit — generated by `Tools/Audit`

Generated 2026-07-16 from `Assets/Scripts/Engine`. **Read-only.**

## Status: best-effort inventory — NOT a privacy proof, NOT claimed complete

Best-effort inventory of **authoritative knowledge-transition candidates**, emitted only from
the authoritative mutation layer (`GameEngine.cs`), used to drive migration onto the ledger
funnel — a **bounded migration aid; completeness is enforced only after encapsulation** (§14.7),
never by this inventory. **This is not a privacy proof.** Privacy is proven at runtime by
`PrivacyTest` (poison scan + paired-world noninterference + typed two-layer boundary): it carries
**no static privacy-field whitelist** — the reachability walk enumerates no fields — but the poison
and decision fixtures still enumerate the threat model and the required decision boundaries.

Four analyzer versions each shipped a completeness claim while blind (helper params → object
initializers → filename-based A/B). **Completeness becomes durable only by ENCAPSULATION**:
making the mutation surface private after migration, so a bypass fails compilation.

- **324** raw sites.
- **324** rows `TODO` on visibility; **`transition_id` grouping NOT implemented** (all `UNGROUPED`).
- Alias tracking is **global and flow-insensitive**; helper summaries understand **bare parameters only**.
